customer_anxiety_profile: Gregory is cooperative and considerate — no hostility or skepticism. His only anxiety is scheduling: he works variable shifts and cannot confirm days off until Friday when the schedule is posted. He cancelled today because he was called in unexpectedly after planning to be home.
unresolved_concerns: Gregory's schedule is not confirmed for next Tuesday — he explicitly said he cannot verify availability until Friday when his work schedule is posted.
No confirmation of whether Gregory will be at a computer and not driving for the appointment.
No 'Is that fair?' commitment anchor obtained — Gregory's acceptance was 'we can try it,' not a firm yes.
sensitive_topics_raised: Gregory works variable/on-call shifts — scheduling around his work calendar is the primary friction point for this appointment holding.
exact_promises_rep_made: Rescheduled for next Tuesday at 2:30 PM (same time as the cancelled appointment).
Told Gregory 'if anything changes, you can just let us know and we can reschedule' — this is an open cancellation invitation the closer should be aware of.
closer_should_know: This appointment is structurally fragile. Gregory said twice he cannot confirm his Tuesday availability until Friday when his work schedule is set. Randy booked Tuesday without addressing that condition and closed with a passive escape hatch. The closer or a confirmation rep should call Gregory on Friday afternoon to verify he is still off Tuesday and re-anchor the appointment with a proper commitment close at that time.
